name: Russia
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **RUSSIA URANIUM-CUSTODIAN TRACK SIDELINED AS PRIMARY MEDIATOR BUT POTENTIALLY REACTIVATED BY MOU.** The 14-point MOU's 'Iran ships out highly-enriched uranium stockpile' provision necessarily requires a custodian — if not Russia, then potentially the IAEA, Kazakhstan (mentioned in earlier-cycle reporting), or US-based facility. Peskov reaffirmed via Pravda EN April 18 (and reiterated to India Today early April) that Russia's offer to take Iran's enriched uranium 'remains active' but the US has rejected the proposal. JPost specifically described the MOU provision as 'Tehran give away enriched uranium' (custodian undefined). The Pakistan track has displaced Russia as the operational mediator. **The 21-day formal-proposal window from prior brief (RESOLVED-NO at Day 9) needs reframing: Russia is sidelined as mediator, BUT could be reactivated as uranium custodian — different role and remains live.** Russia retains structural facets: rhetorical support for Iran via state media (Pravda EN, TASS), intelligence support via permanent communications channel, UNSC veto, Bushehr two new nuclear units commitment. Russian-oil waiver (30-day India extension) preserves ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow to Indian refiners. URA +7.5% partly reflects HEU custodian path legitimization (Cameco 24% of URA, AI power demand intact). Pravda Trump May 6 ran 'IDF preparing large-scale Iran op' single-source claim — REPORTED only, NOT verified mainstream Western media; Russian adversarial-media chain.

active_triggers.condition: Russia announces formal proposal as nuclear-material custodian within 14 days of Putin-Araghchi meeting (by 2026-05-11)

Russia remains an intelligence/diplomatic supporter of Iran | Russia friendly-flag in Iran's three-tier Hormuz system per Bloomberg Tracker — vessels flagged to Russia continue to transit | Russia and China vetoed Bahrain's UNSC resolution to keep Hormuz open (April 7, 11-2 vote, France abstained)
